etymology.description: semantic 火(火) (huǒ, "fire") + phonetic 堯(堯) (yáo, "a legendary emperor; tall") -> boil; burn
etymology.interpretation: 火(火) (huǒ, 'fire') is involved with ignition or heating -> burning -> when combined with 堯(堯) (yáo), which contributes the sound, the character represents the action of boiling or burning where fire is a primary element in producing heat.
etymology.simplification: 燒 was simplified to 烧 by replacing the 呪(呪) (zhòu, "curse") [incorrect as listed] radical 堯(堯) (yáo, "a legendary emperor; tall") with its simplified version 尧(尧).

examples.chinese: 请帮我把水烧开(請幫我把水燒開)。
examples.pinyin: Qǐng bāng wǒ bǎ shuǐ shāo kāi.
examples.english: Please help me boil the water.

examples.chinese: 他不小心把手指烧伤了(他不小心把手指燒傷了)。
examples.pinyin: Tā bù xiǎo xīn bǎ shǒu zhǐ shāo shāng le.

examples.chinese: 这道菜需要烧很长时间(這道菜需要燒很長時間)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè dào cài xū yào shāo hěn cháng shí jiān.
examples.english: This dish needs to be cooked for a long time.
